I think it's awesome. So I just want it, you know, um, you guys are anything like me We're really good at focusing on like where we want to go and what we want to do But sometimes we forget to like look back at like what we've done Um, I mean, I'm like the worst about it with our team We like hit records and I'm like whatever it doesn't matter like there's something else But it's like really important to like recognize these things and um Just at the time of each one of these things that happened at that first event We had five employees the second event we had 18 Now we have 48 and um, it's been in a very short period of time And so, um, I just thought it'd be I thought you guys would find this kind of cool. Um, I think it's kind of cool um But yeah, I like showing this stuff because it shows what we can kind of accomplish as a group Um, and it's really good for like my mental health Because probably like you guys I can only think about what we need to like fix and like all the holes that are wrong in the business Um, but in the past year, we've learned a lot about the gym business and the service business overall most of all from you guys Which made me wonder like what separates this group Um, and why is this group so much more innovative than groups that have been a part of in the past and that I you know know about um, and I think it's because it's this is the only community that i'm aware of that doesn't say like take the easy route and outsource it Um, which is what a lot of people and sometimes licensors franchisors um Doors whatever you want to say like we'll often do that and um, you guys kind of took the hard route which is learn Become better increase your skill set become more valuable treat everyone in your world as special and um, I think that's that's part of why this has become something that's so amazing um and Once a group of entrepreneurs like us With similar challenges come together with skills. There's not much that can really stop us And I think that's that's really predicated on like the fundamental thing that we decided a long time ago Which is that we were going to teach people how to fish and not fish for them and Which by the way just from a business standpoint is like the scariest thing in the entire world because you're like we're just gonna give them everything Like but they could just leave and it's just like scarcity mindset, which is what does that? So it was like literally the scariest thing in the world to do that But it's been the most amazing thing for us to be a part of and witness because The pace of innovation that we have as a community is so much faster than anything that we could ever come up with on our own And most of the cues that we get are from people within the community and we're like that's a great idea Boom let's put structure around it. Let's test it with 20 locations. Let's do it And um, so most of it comes from you guys at this point And so and the only way that that would even be possible Is because everyone here is equipped with the skills to try and know it is possible But if no one knew how to put a pixel and make their own pages Create automation and you know have text follow-ups and email follow-ups and then figure out a way to put text on a Write a name on a whiteboard picture so it automatically changes for a reminder You know what I mean like Unless we have that basic skill set and you guys hadn't taken that first leap of like, okay I'm gonna learn this and confront the fact that I'd have this deficit and skill set But I'm willing to confront it and get better and learn rather than taking the easy way of like, well, I'll just pay an agency I'll just pay someone else to figure it out because at the end of the day Unless you know how to fish Like you're always going to be depending on the fisherman until you become the person who can do it so That is which why gym lunch and gym legacy will always be first and foremost about teaching people how to fish not fishing for you Um, because if the goal really is freedom, I believe it Like how how could anyone say or sell the entrepreneurial dream of freedom? And then in the second like in the same sentence say But you also need to pay us for this one critical business function like lead generation or sales or whatever, right? It just doesn't make sense. And so that's why we structured it the way we did So I just want to thank you guys and if you guys get thank each other For how you guys function within this ecosystem and the extent to which this community has become a giving community And so thank you guys so much. It's because of you guys And Because of that we will fiercely protect this community Um, so I want to thank you for helping protect it and for helping one or not one another out without worry of scarcity um, I've heard just in like the last day probably five stories Of gym owners that are in the same like often the same block who are both gym lords Who found each other here and then like now like meet regularly like uh, Jason schesic and nollaport are like Less than half a mile from each other and they like Strategize together and they plan their campaigns and they like totally go all in on it and guess what they both make way more money And um, I know call barker and tom miller are on the same exact street like and both of them have both increased their revenue Like 3x it's not that like if your gym does well Everyone else's gym has to do bad if you have four gyms that are in a market that I'll do well Everyone makes money. It's just what it is.
